mechanism_narrative: Higher 3-month growth in INDPRO signals recovering production volumes and operating leverage, raising expected corporate earnings and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered INDPRO growth signals produce non-positive average 60-day S&P 500 returns or Rank IC below 0.03 in two consecutive 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS crosses below its level from 3 months earlier by more than 1 percent, household demand contraction lowers revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered observations or the mean triggered return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS combined with non-accelerating CPIAUCSL compresses discount rates without forcing an inflation-risk premium, causing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS 3-month change crosses below -0.25pp while CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation does not rise
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition has lower average 20-day S&P 500 returns than FEDFUNDS declines alone, or if its walk-forward hit rate is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises sharply, labor-market deterioration signals falling income and earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or the downside effect disappears after controlling for prior market return.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in HOUST after prior weakness signals improving construction demand and credit transmission, raising cyclical earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST rebound triggers have average 60-day S&P 500 returns below the unconditional average or a 60-day directional hit rate below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL while FEDFUNDS remains high tightens real household purchasing power and preserves discount-rate pressure, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation crosses above its 12-month inflation rate while FEDFUNDS is above 3%
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction condition is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of observations or if CPI acceleration has no incremental negative effect when FEDFUNDS is high.

mechanism_narrative: A negative quarter-over-quarter change in GDPC1 confirms broad real-demand contraction and lower aggregate profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if GDPC1 contraction triggers have non-negative average 60-day S&P 500 returns or if the signal loses significance in real-time vintages versus revised data.

mechanism_narrative: PAYEMS contraction together with a rising UNRATE identifies synchronized labor-income stress, reducing consumption and earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change crosses below 0 while UNRATE 3-month change is positive
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor-stress trigger is not followed by below-median 5-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of cases or adds no information beyond UNRATE alone.
